title: payment_watcher: block stream broken break re-enters outer loop but skips POLL_INTERVAL before reconnect — watcher loops at 60s even when stream ends normally due to exhaustion vs error conflation
description: In run_watcher_loop, when the inner stream loop exits via break (either Ok(None) stream exhaustion or Err/timeout), execution falls through to the outer loop's sleep(RECONNECT_DELAY_SECS=60). A normally-exhausted stream (Ok(None), all blocks processed) sleeps 60 seconds before reconnecting and checking for new blocks, instead of immediately polling. The intended behavior is: normal stream exhaustion → continue inner loop (check tip again, maybe sleep POLL_INTERVAL=30s); error/timeout → break to outer loop (sleep 60s before reconnect). But all break paths in the stream loop fall to the same outer-loop reconnect sleep. Trigger: normal operation, watcher is caught up — every scan cycle waits 60s instead of 30s. This halves responsiveness to new payment confirmations.
